headline: Your Figma MCP Stack Levels Up
subheadline: Big moves in the Figma-to-Claude pipeline you're actively building, plus a reality check on design leadership in the AI era that's worth sitting with.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Guide to the Figma MCP Server
sections.description: This is Figma's official MCP server documentation — the canonical reference for exactly what you're building at work. It covers the tools within the MCP server, how they bring Figma context into coding workflows, and how to ensure generated code matches both your design system and your designs. Essential bookmark for your white-label sport apps integration.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: How to Connect Claude Code with Figma MCP Fast in 2026
sections.description: Written specifically for designers, not developers — which is refreshing. Walks through the Claude Code ↔ Figma MCP setup step by step. Given you're deep in this exact integration, it's worth scanning for any shortcuts or gotchas you might not have hit yet.

sections.label: Trending
sections.title: Figma March 2026: Code to Figma + AI Design Development MCP
sections.description: Figma just dropped a two-way UI-to-code MCP workflow that works with Cursor, Warp, and others. Plus new AI admin tools for credit management. The two-way sync is huge — this could fundamentally change your handoff story for the white-label system. The Cursor integration alone is directly relevant to your daily stack.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Automating Design Systems with AI: 2026 Workflow Guide
sections.description: Directly addresses your world: using MCP to let Figma send structured design system data to AI tools, automating the repetitive parts so humans focus on decisions. This is the strategic playbook for what you're building — less 'how to set it up' and more 'how to think about the automation layer on top of your token/component architecture.'

sections.label: Watch
sections.title: How I Use (and Don't Use) AI to Design Better in 2026
sections.description: A designer walking through which Figma AI features actually help at each stage of the Double Diamond process — and which ones they skip. Honest and practical. Good for calibrating your own AI-in-design workflow and spotting features you might be underusing or over-hyping.

sections.label: Listen
sections.title: My Guide to Using Lovable + Cursor (The Best Workflow)
sections.description: A walkthrough of syncing code changes both ways between Lovable and Cursor using GitHub as the bridge. You're already using both tools — this could tighten your loop significantly. It's a screen-share video but the narration carries well enough for a stroller walk to get the mental model, then revisit the specifics later.

sections.label: Think about this
sections.title: Design Leaders Are Framing AI as Pressure, Not Opportunity
sections.description: A piece on how product design leaders in 2026 are increasingly experiencing AI as a source of uncertainty and pressure inside their teams — not the exciting capability the industry keeps selling. As someone who's been a Group Head of Design and is now evolving toward product leadership, this is worth sitting with: when you return, your team will likely need you to hold both the optimism and the anxiety. What's your stance?

sections.label: Concept of the day
sections.title: Two-Way MCP: Design ↔ Code as a Living Loop
sections.description: The old world was one-directional: design → handoff → code. Figma's new AI Design Development MCP enables genuine two-way sync — code changes can flow back into editable Figma layers, and design changes push to code. This isn't just a feature; it's a paradigm shift for design systems. Your white-label architecture could become a living system where the source of truth is the *relationship* between Figma and code, not either one alone.
